hypothesis: If tensor completion-based surrogate models (CPD-S, NeAT, and their ensemble) are used to predict Young's modulus and mass-normalized Young's modulus of lattice structures, then they will achieve higher and more robust R² accuracy than Gaussian Process, XGBoost, and MLP baselines under both uniform random and biased (exponentially sampled) training regimes, because tensor completion exploits the multi-modal tensor structure of the design space to generalize from sparse and non-uniform samples.
